scope: public web and X source leads for obvious LLM failures

candidates.failure_description: Model repeatedly claims strawberry has only 2 r's instead of 3 due to tokenization.
candidates.human_triviality: Literate adult can count the three r's by spelling the word: s-t-r-a-w-b-e-r-r-y.
candidates.citation_title: Incorrect count of 'r' characters in the word "strawberry"
candidates.notes: Direct bug report with user screenshots; reproduces across GPT models; classic tokenization example.

candidates.original_prompt: how to make cheese stick to pizza
candidates.claimed_output: add non-toxic glue to the sauce
candidates.failure_description: AI Overview recommends adding glue to pizza sauce for cheese adhesion.
candidates.human_triviality: No adult would add glue to food; basic safety常识 prevents it.
candidates.citation_title: Google AI search tells users to glue pizza and eat rocks
candidates.notes: Viral example with embedded user screenshots; also covered eating rocks; high public visibility.

candidates.original_prompt: How many times does the letter “r” appear in the word “strawberry”?
candidates.failure_description: Multiple frontier models fail to count r's correctly due to tokenization ignoring character level.
candidates.human_triviality: Simple spelling and counting exercise taught in early education.
candidates.notes: Explains mechanism; quotes model outputs; reproducible on older snapshots.

candidates.original_prompt: how many r's are in strawberry?
candidates.failure_description: Top models in ranking all fail strawberry r-count until prompted with character breaks.
candidates.human_triviality: Child-level task of enumerating letters in a familiar word.
candidates.citation_title: All 3 models you ranked cannot get "how many r's are in strawberry?"
candidates.notes: Community discussion with token breakdown examples; strong evidence of widespread failure.

candidates.original_prompt: cheese not sticking to pizza / eat rocks
candidates.claimed_output: non-toxic glue; one rock per day
candidates.failure_description: AI Overviews hallucinates dangerous or absurd advice from misinterpreted sources.
candidates.human_triviality: Basic常识: glue and rocks are not food ingredients or health advice.
candidates.citation_title: Google's AI Recommends Glue on Pizza: What Caused These Viral Blunders?
candidates.notes: Cites X posts with screenshots; multiple related absurd outputs.

rejected_or_weak_leads.lead: Recent X posts referencing strawberry test in 2026
rejected_or_weak_leads.reason: Mostly commentary or jokes; no new direct model output screenshots with stable verifiable URLs beyond known cases.

rejected_or_weak_leads.lead: Reddit threads on general word/letter counting
rejected_or_weak_leads.reason: Many anecdotal but lack specific model, date-stamped prompts/outputs with citable direct evidence.

search_notes: Strawberry r-count and Google glue-pizza dominate verifiable public sources (high-confidence direct examples from forums, news, HN). Covered major queries; additional variants (e.g., other words like congratulations, simple math) appear in discussions but require reproduction for new candidates. 5 strong candidates extracted; more could be added via targeted model testing on public examples.
